behavior_summary: This module is a single-clock synchronous FIFO with depth 13 and data width 8. A push is accepted on a rising edge when push_valid and push_ready are both 1. A pop is accepted on a rising edge when pop_valid and pop_ready are both 1. When the FIFO is empty, it supports combinational bypass: if push_valid=1 and pop_ready=1, then pop_valid is asserted and pop_data equals push_data in the same cycle, providing zero-cycle transfer through the empty FIFO. Otherwise, when data is stored internally, pop_data/pop_valid are driven combinationally from the current head entry of the FIFO storage. Stored entries preserve FIFO ordering. If the FIFO is non-empty, popping removes the head entry at the clock edge and the next queued item becomes visible on pop_data on the following cycle. When empty and pop_ready=0, a valid push is not bypassed; it is stored for later delivery. Status outputs report current occupancy/fullness and next-cycle predicted occupancy/fullness based on pending accepted push/pop transactions.
edge_cases: At depth 0, if push_valid=1 and pop_ready=1, the transfer bypasses storage and pop_data must equal push_data combinationally in that same cycle.
At depth 0, if push_valid=1 and pop_ready=0, the item must be buffered rather than bypassed.
A push is accepted only when push_valid && push_ready; if full, push_ready must be 0 and no push may be accepted.
A pop occurs only when pop_valid && pop_ready; if empty and no bypass condition makes data available, pop_valid must be 0 and no pop may be accepted.
Simultaneous accepted push and accepted pop should leave occupancy unchanged while still preserving FIFO ordering.
When non-empty, the popped item is the current head entry; the next stored item appears on pop_data on the following clock cycle, not necessarily combinationally in the same cycle after the pop edge.
Occupancy is bounded to 0..13; items must not underflow below 0 or overflow above 13.
slots is the complement of items relative to capacity 13, so slots must remain bounded to 0..13.
On reset, the FIFO becomes empty, push_ready=1, pop_valid=0, full=0, empty=1, items=0, slots=13.

The spec states next-state flags are based on current state plus pending push/pop transactions, but exact same-cycle behavior of *_next during bypass/reset edges is not fully spelled out and should be treated carefully.
state_transitions: RESET state on rising clk with rst=1: occupancy becomes 0 and status reflects empty FIFO.
EMPTY -> EMPTY when no accepted push occurs.
EMPTY -> NONEMPTY when a push is accepted but not consumed purely through empty-bypass storage-elision.
EMPTY with push_valid=1 and pop_ready=1 supports direct transfer in the same cycle; externally a pop transaction occurs immediately via bypass.
NONEMPTY -> NONEMPTY when neither push nor pop is accepted, or when both are accepted in the same cycle.
NONEMPTY -> more occupied when push is accepted and pop is not accepted.
NONEMPTY -> less occupied when pop is accepted and push is not accepted.
NONEMPTY -> EMPTY when exactly one stored item exists and that item is popped without a simultaneous accepted push replacing occupancy.
priorities: rst has highest priority over normal FIFO operation.
Handshake qualification has priority over raw valid/ready assertions: transfers occur only on valid && ready.
When empty and both push_valid and pop_ready are high, bypass behavior takes priority over presenting no data.
A full FIFO must block pushes by deasserting push_ready.
An empty FIFO without bypass opportunity must block pops by deasserting pop_valid.
mutation_risks: Bypass implemented incorrectly when empty, especially failing to drive pop_data=push_data combinationally in the same cycle.
Bypass conditioned on wrong signals, e.g. requiring internal state update first or ignoring pop_ready.
Incorrect behavior for empty + push_valid + !pop_ready, where data should be stored rather than lost or falsely popped.
Off-by-one errors in occupancy/count/status updates, especially around transitions 0<->1 and 12<->13.
Incorrect full or empty flag generation at capacity boundaries.
Incorrect handling of simultaneous push and pop, especially occupancy changing by +/-1 instead of staying constant.
Incorrect FIFO ordering after mixed push/pop sequences.
pop_data updated one cycle late or too early after a pop from buffered mode.
push_ready derived incorrectly during full or nearly-full conditions.
pop_valid derived incorrectly during empty, bypass, or single-entry conditions.
slots not equal to 13-items, or counters sized/updated incorrectly.
executable_reference: Treat accepted_push as (push_valid && push_ready) and accepted_pop as (pop_valid && pop_ready).
On any rising edge with rst=1, the reference occupancy becomes 0; afterward expect empty=1, full=0, items=0, slots=13, pop_valid=0, push_ready=1.
Maintain a reference queue of accepted pushed bytes that were not consumed via same-cycle empty bypass.
If the reference queue is non-empty, expect pop_valid=1 and expect pop_data to equal the oldest queued byte.
If the reference queue is empty and push_valid=1 and pop_ready=1 and rst=0, expect pop_valid=1 and pop_data=push_data in that same cycle.
If the reference queue is empty and bypass condition is false, expect pop_valid=0.
When the queue is full (13 stored items), expect push_ready=0; otherwise expect push_ready=1.
When an accepted pop occurs from buffered data, remove exactly the oldest queued item at the active clock edge.
When an accepted push occurs while not in empty-bypass pass-through-only behavior, append push_data to the tail of the reference queue at the active clock edge.
For simultaneous accepted push and accepted pop in buffered mode, expect the popped value to be the pre-edge head item and preserve FIFO order for subsequent outputs.
Expect items to equal current stored queue depth and slots to equal 13 minus that depth.
Check full iff items==13 and empty iff items==0.
Check *_next only as one-step lookahead of occupancy/status derived from current visible state and the currently pending accepted transactions when that relationship is unambiguous.
guaranteed_properties: The module is a synchronous single-clock FIFO using clk.
rst is synchronous and active high.
FIFO depth is 13 entries and data width is 8 bits.
push transactions are accepted only when push_valid and push_ready are both high on a clock cycle.
pop transactions occur only when pop_valid and pop_ready are both high on a clock cycle.
push_ready is asserted whenever the FIFO is not full.
pop_valid is asserted whenever the FIFO contains at least one valid item.
When the FIFO is empty and push_valid=1 and pop_ready=1, pop_valid is asserted combinationally and pop_data equals push_data in the same cycle.
When the FIFO is not empty, data is output in FIFO order from the head of internal storage.
After a buffered pop, the next queued item becomes available on pop_data on the following clock cycle.
pop_data and pop_valid are not driven by a dedicated final register stage; they are combinationally driven from bypass inputs or internal memory read output.
On reset, externally visible empty/full/items/slots/push_ready/pop_valid reflect an empty FIFO: empty=1, full=0, items=0, slots=13, push_ready=1, pop_valid=0.
ambiguous_properties: Exact port directions and bit widths are inferred from the reference RTL header and FIFO capacity, not directly enumerated in the prose spec for every status signal.
The exact combinational truth table for full_next, empty_next, items_next, and slots_next during all corner cases is not completely spelled out cycle-by-cycle.
The precise meaning of 'currently stored' versus inclusion of same-cycle bypass-only traffic in items/items_next is not fully formalized in the prose.
Whether empty/full/items/slots are purely current-state combinational decodes of registered occupancy or include any same-cycle combinational adjustments is not explicitly stated.
Behavior of pop_data when pop_valid=0 is unspecified; it may hold a stale/internal value and should not be strictly checked unless pop_valid=1.

Same-cycle interaction when the FIFO holds one item and also sees a simultaneous push and pop may permit several equivalent implementations of internal pointer movement as long as visible FIFO semantics hold.
The exact visibility timing of next-state outputs during rst=1 versus immediately after the active edge is not fully specified.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume pop_data has a meaningful value whenever pop_valid=0.
Do not assume exact internal pointer encoding, storage implementation, or whether memory write/read share addresses in any specific way.

Do not assume bypass traffic increments items if the transfer is accepted directly through an empty FIFO unless the implementation clearly exposes that behavior; check the externally guaranteed handshake/result instead.
Do not assume an exact cycle-accurate formula for *_next beyond clear one-step lookahead intent unless validated by the explicit spec.
Do not assume same-cycle combinational availability of the next buffered item immediately after a pop edge; the spec explicitly says following clock cycle.
Do not assume outputs other than the listed reset-visible flags are forced to any particular value during reset, especially pop_data.
Do not assume acceptance of push when full or pop when empty, even if opposite-side handshake is asserted in the same cycle, except where explicit empty-bypass behavior is guaranteed.
Do not assume any latency or timing guarantee beyond the explicit empty-FIFO cut-through case and the stated following-cycle availability after buffered pops.
